prompt: Create a luxurious Japanese magazine-style PR editorial spread for a publishing and branding service, designed as a two-page brochure layout. The left half is a dark, cinematic portrait scene in a sophisticated office lounge: a seated Japanese businessman in a navy bespoke suit, white shirt, dark tie, white pocket square, and wristwatch sits in a leather chair with one hand near his chin, posed thoughtfully beside a glossy wooden table. His face is intentionally obscured with a soft rectangular blur. The lighting is moody and warm, with reflections from tall windows and a premium executive atmosphere. On this dark left page, place large elegant serif headline text in English reading PUBLISHING BRANDING, stacked in two lines near the upper left in muted gold. Below it, add multiple lines of Japanese copy in white, including a larger subheadline and several short body paragraphs arranged vertically. Near the lower left, include a bordered dark box containing exactly 4 checklist items, each preceded by a check mark, with Japanese text. Put a small page number "02" in the bottom-left corner. The right half is a clean white editorial information page with refined minimalist typography and generous spacing. At the top, place a large Japanese headline in black, aligned upper center-right, with two short Japanese body text blocks beneath it; on the upper far right include a small photo of exactly 4 stacked hardcover books and a glass vase near a bright window with greenery. Across the middle, add a centered section title in Japanese for five benefits, followed by exactly 5 numbered columns labeled 1 through 5, each with a short Japanese heading and several lines of smaller explanatory Japanese text. Beneath that, add another centered Japanese section title for workflow, followed by exactly 4 step blocks laid out horizontally: STEP 01 with a speech bubble icon, STEP 02 with a pencil icon, STEP 03 with an open book icon, STEP 04 with a megaphone icon. Each step includes the English word STEP plus a two-digit number, a short Japanese subtitle, and a few lines of smaller Japanese description, with small chevrons between steps. Along the bottom of the right page, place exactly 3 rectangular photos side by side: a city-view office meeting table scene, a close-up of a hand writing with a pen, and a stack of books near an open book. Under these images, add a prominent Japanese closing statement in a serif style on the lower left, and a smaller Japanese paragraph on the lower right. Put page number "03" in the bottom-right corner. Use a premium palette of deep navy, black, warm brown, ivory, and muted gold, with a balance of editorial luxury, corporate trust, and high-end publishing aesthetics. The overall composition should feel like a real magazine advertisement or consulting brochure, polished, photorealistic, elegant, and credible.
